description: GitSlop — A free, open-source, cross-platform GUI git client built with Electron + React + TypeScript. Rivals GitKraken with visual branch graphs, inline diffs, built-in editor, toggleable CLI, and full git operation support.

userStories.title: Electron App Shell & Window Management
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the foundational Electron + React + TypeScript app scaffold so all other features have a shell to live in.
acceptanceCriteria: Electron app boots and shows a React-rendered window
Window supports minimize, maximize, close, and resize
App has a custom titlebar with GitSlop branding
Main process and renderer process are separated correctly
Hot reload works in development mode
TypeScript strict mode enabled, builds without errors
npm run dev starts the app, npm run build produces a distributable
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Foundation story — everything else depends on this. Use electron-forge or electron-vite for scaffolding. React 18+, TypeScript strict.

userStories.title: Application Layout & Navigation Shell
userStories.description: As a user, I want a clear application layout so I can navigate between different views and panels.
acceptanceCriteria: Left sidebar with collapsible sections (Branches, Remotes, Tags, Stashes)
Center panel area for main content (graph, diff, editor)
Bottom panel area for toggleable CLI terminal
Top toolbar with action buttons (pull, push, fetch, branch, stash, etc.)
Panels are resizable via drag handles
Layout state persists across restarts
Responsive — panels collapse gracefully at small window sizes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use CSS Grid or flexbox layout. Consider allotment or react-resizable-panels for drag handles. Persist layout to electron-store.

userStories.title: Welcome Screen & Recent Repos
userStories.description: As a user, I want a welcome screen when no repo is open so I can quickly open, clone, or init a repository.
acceptanceCriteria: Welcome screen shown when no repo is open
Open Repository button that launches a native folder picker
Clone Repository button that opens a clone dialog (placeholder for now)
Init Repository button that opens a folder picker and inits a new repo
List of recently opened repositories persisted across sessions
Clicking a recent repo opens it immediately
Recent repos show path and last-opened date
Remove individual entries from recent repos list
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use Electron dialog.showOpenDialog for folder picker. Store recent repos in electron-store.

userStories.title: Git CLI Wrapper Service
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a service layer that executes git commands via the user's installed git CLI and parses output into structured data.
acceptanceCriteria: Service runs git commands via child_process.execFile in the main process
IPC bridge exposes git operations to the renderer process
Handles errors gracefully: git not installed, not a repo, auth failures
Commands are queued to prevent concurrent git operations on the same repo
Supports cancellation of long-running operations via AbortController
Returns structured data (parsed objects), not raw strings
Detects user's git version on startup and warns if too old (< 2.20)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Core infrastructure. Use git --format flags for structured output where possible. All git interaction goes through this service.

userStories.title: Open & Init Repositories
userStories.description: As a user, I want to open an existing git repo or initialize a new one so I can start working.
acceptanceCriteria: Open Repo opens a native folder picker dialog
If selected folder is a git repo, load it into the app
If selected folder is NOT a git repo, show error with option to init
Init Repo creates a new git repo in the selected folder via git init
After opening/initing, app transitions from welcome screen to repo view
Repo path shown in titlebar
Recent repos list updated on open
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-004 (git service) and US-003 (welcome screen). Use git rev-parse --git-dir to verify a folder is a repo.

userStories.title: Clone Repository Dialog
userStories.description: As a user, I want to clone a remote repository so I can start working on an existing project.
acceptanceCriteria: Clone dialog with fields: URL, destination folder, name (auto-filled from URL)
Supports HTTPS and SSH URLs
Browse button to pick destination folder
Progress indicator showing clone progress
Cancel button to abort clone
On success, automatically opens the cloned repo
On failure, shows clear error message (auth failed, network error, etc.)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use git clone --progress and parse stderr for progress updates.

userStories.title: Commit Graph — Data & Rendering
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a visual branch/commit graph so I can understand the project's history at a glance.
acceptanceCriteria: Parse git log --all into structured commit data with parent relationships
Render a visual commit graph with colored branch lines using Canvas or SVG
Each commit row shows: short hash, message (first line), author, relative date
Branch and tag labels rendered as badges on their respective commits
HEAD indicator clearly visible
Graph renders correctly for merge commits and linear history
Virtualised scrolling — handles repos with 100k+ commits without lag
Graph auto-refreshes when the repo changes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Most complex UI story. Use react-window or react-virtuoso for virtualisation. Consider Canvas for graph lines (SVG may be too slow for large repos). Use git log --format to get structured data.

userStories.title: Commit Graph — Interaction & Selection
userStories.description: As a user, I want to interact with the commit graph to view details and perform actions on commits.
acceptanceCriteria: Click a commit to select it and show details in a side/bottom panel
Commit detail panel shows: full hash, author, date, full message, list of changed files
Right-click a commit for context menu (cherry-pick, revert, reset, create branch, create tag, copy SHA)
Double-click a file in commit details to show its diff
Keyboard navigation: arrow keys to move between commits
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-007 (graph rendering). Use git show --stat for commit details. Context menu actions are placeholders until their respective stories.

userStories.title: Branch Sidebar — List & Management
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see and manage all branches from the sidebar so I can switch contexts easily.
acceptanceCriteria: Sidebar section Branches lists all local branches via git branch
Current branch highlighted/bolded
Double-click a branch to check it out
Right-click context menu: rename, delete, merge into current, rebase onto current, push
New Branch button opens dialog: branch name, base branch/commit
Branches sorted alphabetically with current branch pinned to top
Search/filter box to find branches in large repos
Show ahead/behind count relative to tracking branch
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use git branch -vv --format for structured branch data including tracking info.

userStories.title: Remote Branches & Remote Management
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see remote branches and manage remotes so I can collaborate with others.
acceptanceCriteria: Sidebar section Remotes lists all remotes, expandable to show their branches
Double-click a remote branch to check it out as a local tracking branch
Right-click remote branch: delete remote branch, fetch
Add Remote dialog: name, URL
Edit Remote dialog: change URL
Remove Remote with confirmation
Fetch all or fetch individual remotes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use git remote -v and git branch -r for data.

userStories.title: Tags Sidebar
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see and manage tags so I can mark releases and important commits.
acceptanceCriteria: Sidebar section Tags lists all tags
Click a tag to scroll the graph to that commit
Right-click: delete tag, push tag
New Tag dialog: name, target commit (defaults to HEAD), message (for annotated tags)
Tags sorted by date (newest first)
Search/filter tags
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use git tag --sort=-creatordate --format for structured output.

userStories.title: Stash Sidebar
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see and manage stashes so I can temporarily shelve work.
acceptanceCriteria: Sidebar section Stashes lists all stashes with message and date
Click a stash to view its diff
Right-click: apply, pop, drop
Stash button in toolbar: stash all changes, with optional message
Option to include untracked files in stash
Stash apply shows conflicts if any
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use git stash list --format for structured output.

userStories.title: Working Directory Status Panel
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see the current status of my working directory — staged, unstaged, and untracked files.
acceptanceCriteria: Status panel shows three sections: Staged, Unstaged, Untracked
Each file shows: filename, path, change type icon (added/modified/deleted/renamed)
File counts shown in section headers
Click a file to show its diff in the diff viewer
Status auto-refreshes on file system changes via file watcher
Empty state messaging when working directory is clean
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use git status --porcelain=v2 for machine-parseable status output.

userStories.title: Staging & Unstaging Files
userStories.description: As a user, I want to stage and unstage files so I can prepare my commits precisely.
acceptanceCriteria: Stage button (+ icon) on each unstaged/untracked file
Unstage button (- icon) on each staged file
Stage All button to stage everything
Unstage All button to unstage everything
Drag and drop files between staged and unstaged sections
Keyboard shortcut to stage/unstage selected file(s)
Multi-select files with Ctrl/Shift+click for bulk stage/unstage
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use git add and git restore --staged for stage/unstage.

userStories.title: Hunk & Line Staging
userStories.description: As a user, I want to stage individual hunks or lines so I can make precise, atomic commits.
acceptanceCriteria: When viewing diff of an unstaged file, each hunk has a Stage Hunk button
Individual lines within a hunk can be selected and staged
Staged hunks/lines move to the staged diff view
Unstage Hunk button on staged hunks
Visual indicators clearly distinguish staged vs unstaged portions of a file
Works correctly for added, modified, and deleted files
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use git apply --cached with patch data for partial staging. Generate patches from selected hunks/lines.

userStories.title: Diff Viewer — Side-by-Side & Inline
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see file diffs with syntax highlighting in both side-by-side and inline modes.
acceptanceCriteria: Toggle between side-by-side and inline (unified) diff views
Syntax highlighting for common languages (JS/TS, Python, Rust, Go, Java, C/C++, HTML, CSS, JSON, YAML, Markdown, shell)
Line numbers shown for both old and new versions
Added lines highlighted green, removed highlighted red, modified with word-level diff
Word-level diff highlighting within changed lines
Scroll sync between left and right panes in side-by-side mode
Handle binary files gracefully (show Binary file changed message)
Handle large files gracefully (truncate with Show more option)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Consider using Monaco's built-in diff editor for this. Or use diff2html for rendering. Syntax highlighting via Prism or Monaco.

userStories.title: Commit Dialog
userStories.description: As a user, I want to write a commit message and commit my staged changes.
acceptanceCriteria: Commit message input: subject line + expandable body textarea
Character count indicator on subject line (warn at 72 chars)
Commit button (disabled when no staged changes or empty message)
Commit & Push button for convenience
Amend checkbox to amend the last commit (pre-fills message from previous commit)
Sign-off checkbox (appends Signed-off-by line)
Keyboard shortcut to commit (Ctrl+Enter)
After commit, clear message and refresh status panel
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use git commit -m for basic commit. Use git commit --amend for amend. Use git log -1 --format=%B to pre-fill amend message.

userStories.title: Push, Pull, Fetch Operations
userStories.description: As a user, I want to push, pull, and fetch with clear feedback so I can sync with remotes.
acceptanceCriteria: Toolbar buttons for Push, Pull, Fetch
Push: pushes current branch to its tracking remote
Pull: pulls with configurable strategy (merge or rebase)
Fetch: fetches all remotes
Progress indicator during operations
Force push option with confirmation dialog warning about data loss
Error handling: auth failure, network error, rejected push
Push dialog for setting upstream when branch has no tracking branch
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use git push --progress, git pull --progress, git fetch --all --progress. Parse stderr for progress.

userStories.title: Merge
userStories.description: As a user, I want to merge branches visually so I can integrate changes.
acceptanceCriteria: Right-click branch in sidebar → Merge into current branch
Merge dialog with branch picker accessible from toolbar
Preview: show how many commits will be merged
Fast-forward merge when possible (configurable)
If conflicts arise, transition to merge conflict resolution view
Abort merge button available during conflict resolution
Success notification after clean merge
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use git merge. Use git rev-list --count to preview merge size. Use git merge --abort for abort.

userStories.title: Rebase
userStories.description: As a user, I want to rebase branches so I can maintain clean linear history.
acceptanceCriteria: Right-click branch → Rebase current onto this branch
Interactive rebase: show list of commits with pick/squash/edit/drop options
If conflicts arise during rebase, show conflict resolution view with continue/abort/skip
Progress indicator showing which commit is being replayed
Abort rebase button always accessible
Warning when rebasing published commits
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Interactive rebase is complex. Use GIT_SEQUENCE_EDITOR env var to control rebase-todo. Use git rebase --continue/--abort/--skip.

userStories.title: Cherry-Pick
userStories.description: As a user, I want to cherry-pick commits so I can selectively apply changes.
acceptanceCriteria: Right-click commit in graph → Cherry-pick
Multi-select commits for batch cherry-pick
If conflicts, show conflict resolution view
Success notification with link to new commit
Abort cherry-pick option
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use git cherry-pick. Use git cherry-pick --abort for abort.

userStories.title: Reset (Soft, Mixed, Hard)
userStories.description: As a user, I want to reset my branch to a previous commit with different modes.
acceptanceCriteria: Right-click commit in graph → Reset current branch to here
Dialog with mode selection: Soft, Mixed (default), Hard
Clear explanation of what each mode does in the dialog
Hard reset requires explicit confirmation (type HARD to confirm)
After reset, refresh graph and status panel
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use git reset --soft/--mixed/--hard. Hard reset is destructive — confirmation is critical.

userStories.title: Revert Commit
userStories.description: As a user, I want to revert a commit so I can undo changes safely without rewriting history.
acceptanceCriteria: Right-click commit in graph → Revert
Creates a new revert commit
If conflicts, show conflict resolution view
Revert of merge commits: prompt for parent number
Success notification
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use git revert. Use git revert -m <parent> for merge commits.

userStories.title: Merge Conflict Resolution Tool
userStories.description: As a user, I want a visual 3-way merge tool so I can resolve conflicts without leaving the app.
acceptanceCriteria: 3-pane view: Ours (left), Theirs (right), Result (bottom)
Syntax highlighting in all panes
Conflict markers highlighted and clickable
Accept Ours, Accept Theirs, Accept Both buttons per conflict
Manual editing in the Result pane
Navigation: Next Conflict / Previous Conflict buttons
Conflict count indicator (e.g. Conflict 2 of 5)
Mark as Resolved button per file
After all files resolved, prompt to continue merge/rebase/cherry-pick
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use Monaco editor for all three panes. Parse conflict markers from files. Use git checkout --ours/--theirs for bulk resolution. Use git add to mark resolved.

userStories.title: Built-in Code Editor
userStories.description: As a user, I want a built-in code editor so I can make quick edits without switching to an external editor.
acceptanceCriteria: Monaco editor embedded in a tab/panel
Open files from the file tree, diff viewer, or commit details
Syntax highlighting for common languages
File tabs for multiple open files
Modified indicator on unsaved tabs
Save with Ctrl+S
Undo/redo support
Line numbers, minimap, word wrap toggle
Saving a file triggers status panel refresh
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use @monaco-editor/react. Lazy-load Monaco to reduce initial bundle size.

userStories.title: Toggleable CLI Terminal
userStories.description: As a power user, I want a built-in terminal so I can run git commands or any shell commands without leaving the app.
acceptanceCriteria: Terminal panel at the bottom, toggled with keyboard shortcut (Ctrl+`)
Uses xterm.js for terminal emulation
Starts user's default shell (bash/zsh/cmd/powershell) in the repo directory
Multiple terminal tabs
Resizable panel height via drag handle
Terminal output triggers repo status refresh (debounced)
Copy/paste works correctly
Font size matches app theme settings
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use xterm.js + node-pty. node-pty requires native compilation — ensure electron-rebuild is configured.

userStories.title: Search — Commits, Files, Branches
userStories.description: As a user, I want to search across commits, files, and branches so I can find what I'm looking for quickly.
acceptanceCriteria: Global search bar (Ctrl+K) with command-palette style dropdown
Search modes: Commits (message, SHA, author), Files (filename), Branches/Tags
Results displayed in a filterable dropdown
Selecting a commit result scrolls graph to it
Selecting a file result opens it in the editor
Selecting a branch result checks it out (with confirmation)
Search is debounced and fast (< 200ms for local results)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use git log --grep for commit search, git ls-files for file search. Consider cmdk library for command palette UI.

userStories.title: Commit History & Filtering
userStories.description: As a user, I want to filter and explore commit history so I can find specific changes.
acceptanceCriteria: Filter by: author, date range, file path, commit message text
Filters combinable (e.g. author + date range)
Filter UI as a collapsible bar above the graph
Active filters shown as removable chips/badges
Show commits touching this file from file context menu
Filtered view clearly indicates it is filtered (not the full history)
Clear all filters button
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use git log --author, --since/--until, -- <path>, --grep flags for filtering.

userStories.title: File Tree View
userStories.description: As a user, I want to browse the repository's file tree so I can navigate the project structure.
acceptanceCriteria: File tree in a sidebar tab (alongside branch sidebar)
Expandable/collapsible folder hierarchy
File icons by type/extension
Right-click: open in editor, show history, show blame, copy path
Double-click to open in built-in editor
Git status indicators on files (modified, staged, untracked, conflicted)
Search/filter within the file tree
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use git ls-files and git status --porcelain for tree + status data. Use a file icon library like file-icons or vscode-icons.

userStories.title: Git Blame View
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see git blame annotations so I can understand who changed each line and when.
acceptanceCriteria: Blame gutter alongside file content in the editor view
Shows author, date, and commit SHA per line block
Hover on blame annotation shows full commit message
Click blame annotation to view that commit in the graph
Color-code blame blocks by age or author
Toggle blame on/off
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use git blame --porcelain for structured blame data.

userStories.title: Theme System — Dark & Light
userStories.description: As a user, I want to choose between dark and light themes so I can work comfortably in any lighting.
acceptanceCriteria: Dark theme (default) and light theme fully implemented
Toggle in settings and via keyboard shortcut
All UI elements styled correctly in both themes (no broken/unreadable sections)
Theme preference persisted across sessions
Smooth transition when switching themes
Consistent color palette — professional, not garish
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use CSS custom properties for theming. Define a design token system. Consider VS Code's color palette as inspiration.

userStories.title: Keyboard Shortcuts System
userStories.description: As a user, I want comprehensive keyboard shortcuts so I can work without touching the mouse.
acceptanceCriteria: Default shortcuts for all common actions (commit, push, pull, stage, etc.)
Keyboard shortcut reference panel accessible via Ctrl+?
Shortcuts shown in tooltips and context menus
No conflicting shortcuts
Shortcuts: Ctrl+Enter (commit), Ctrl+Shift+P (push), Ctrl+Shift+L (pull), Ctrl+Shift+F (fetch), Ctrl+` (terminal), Ctrl+K (search), Ctrl+B (sidebar), Ctrl+S (save), Ctrl+Z/Y (undo/redo)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use a central shortcut registry. Consider mousetrap or hotkeys-js library.

userStories.title: Settings & Preferences Panel
userStories.description: As a user, I want a settings panel to configure the application to my liking.
acceptanceCriteria: Settings accessible from menu and keyboard shortcut (Ctrl+,)
Sections: General, Appearance, Git, Editor, Keybindings
General: default clone directory, auto-fetch interval, proxy settings
Appearance: theme (dark/light), font family, font size, sidebar position
Git: default pull strategy (merge/rebase), sign commits (GPG), auto-stash on pull, default branch name
Editor: tab size, word wrap, minimap on/off
Settings stored in a JSON config file in user app data directory
Changes take effect immediately (no restart required)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use electron-store for persistence. Settings UI as a full-page view or modal.

userStories.title: Notifications & Status Bar
userStories.description: As a user, I want clear feedback about operations so I know what's happening.
acceptanceCriteria: Status bar at the bottom showing: current branch, sync status (ahead/behind), active operation
Toast notifications for completed operations (commit, push, pull, etc.)
Error notifications with Show Details expansion
Notification history accessible from status bar
Loading spinners on toolbar buttons during async operations
Operation progress bar for clone, fetch, push, pull
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Consider react-hot-toast or sonner for toast notifications.

userStories.title: Auto-Fetch & File Watching
userStories.description: As a user, I want the app to stay up-to-date automatically so I always see the current state.
acceptanceCriteria: File system watcher detects changes in the working directory using chokidar
Status panel and diff viewer auto-refresh on file changes (debounced at 300ms)
Auto-fetch from remotes on a configurable interval (default: 5 minutes)
Auto-fetch can be disabled in settings
Incoming changes indicator in status bar after fetch
Refresh button for manual full reload
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use chokidar for file watching. Ignore .git directory changes except for refs.

userStories.title: Context Menus Throughout
userStories.description: As a user, I want right-click context menus on all interactive elements for quick access to actions.
acceptanceCriteria: Context menu on commits: cherry-pick, revert, reset, create branch, create tag, copy SHA
Context menu on branches: checkout, merge, rebase, rename, delete, push
Context menu on files (status): stage, unstage, discard changes, open in editor, show history
Context menu on remotes: fetch, edit, remove
Context menu on tags: checkout, delete, push
Context menu on stashes: apply, pop, drop
All context menu items show keyboard shortcuts where applicable
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Create a reusable ContextMenu component. Wire up to existing action handlers from earlier stories.

userStories.title: Discard Changes
userStories.description: As a user, I want to discard changes to files so I can revert unwanted modifications.
acceptanceCriteria: Right-click file → Discard Changes with confirmation dialog
Discard All Changes button with confirmation
Discard individual hunks from the diff viewer
Confirmation dialog clearly states the action is irreversible
Works for modified, deleted, and new (untracked) files
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use git checkout -- <file> for tracked files. Use rm for untracked files. Use git apply --reverse for hunk discard.

userStories.title: Submodule Support
userStories.description: As a user, I want basic submodule support so I can work with repos that use them.
acceptanceCriteria: Sidebar shows submodules section when present
Submodule status: initialized, dirty, out of date
Init/update submodule from context menu
Open submodule as a separate repo in a new window
Submodule changes visible in status panel
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use git submodule status and git submodule foreach for data.

userStories.title: GPG Commit Signing
userStories.description: As a user, I want to sign my commits with GPG so I can verify authorship.
acceptanceCriteria: Setting to enable commit signing by default
GPG key selection in settings (reads from git config or lists available keys)
Signed commits shown with a verification badge in the graph
Sign individual commits via checkbox in commit dialog
Clear error message if GPG key is not found or passphrase fails
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use git commit -S for signing. Use git log --show-signature to detect signed commits. Use gpg --list-secret-keys to list available keys.

userStories.title: Cross-Platform Packaging & Distribution
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the app to build and package correctly on Linux, macOS, and Windows.
acceptanceCriteria: Electron Builder configured for Linux (.deb, .AppImage), macOS (.dmg), Windows (.exe, .msi)
Auto-update mechanism via electron-updater
App icon and branding on all platforms
Code signing configured (placeholder for future certificates)
README with build instructions for all platforms
CI pipeline config (GitHub Actions) for automated builds on all platforms
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use electron-builder. Configure GitHub Actions matrix build for linux/mac/windows.
